# branch squash can opt out of automatic upstack restacking.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-07-09T12:05:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Create a branch with two commits and an upstack branch.
git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature1 -m 'First feature1 commit'
git add feature1b.txt
git commit -m 'Second feature1 commit'
git add feature2.txt
gs branch create feature2 -m 'Add feature2'

git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

gs down
gs branch squash --no-restack --message 'Squashed feature1'

# Squashing rewrites feature1; feature2 stays at its original head only when
# automatic restacking is disabled.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

# Config can disable restacking by default, and --restack=upstack can override
# the configured value for a single squash.
git reset --hard HEAD@{1}
git config spice.branchSquash.restack none
git add feature1c.txt
git commit -m 'Third feature1 commit'
gs branch squash --restack=upstack --message 'Squashed feature1 again'
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/flag-upstack-graph.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
Feature 1
-- repo/feature1b.txt --
Feature 1b
-- repo/feature1c.txt --
Feature 1c
-- repo/feature2.txt --
Feature 2
-- golden/feature2-before.txt --
efe0fd9
-- golden/flag-upstack-graph.txt --
* 94c3715 (feature2) Add feature2
* 7dec11a (HEAD -> feature1) Squashed feature1 again
* 85f590c (main) Initial commit
